evaluator_feedback.feedback: Iteration 1 delivered the severity-anchor fix cleanly and correctly: the Blocker/High/Medium/Nit rubric now carries behavioral anchors tied to the stated core_journey and business_goal, explicitly forbids 'High' as a default, requires Nielsen's frequency/magnitude/persistence triad to be reconstructible from impact_on_user + impact_on_business, and the Reflection Loop enforces a blocker-cap and downgrade discipline. finding_severity_discipline jumps 2.0 → 4.0. The gemini.py error classification + exponential backoff work is outside the review-quality criteria and correctly scoped. However, two of three active critical criteria received no direct fix: persona_grounding_and_voice is flat (no voice reminder near Execution Instructions / Reflection Loop), and evidence_grounding only got the plain-text-fallback competitor-rule echo plus temperature tightening — the big structural lever (threading webpage_context through run_review + substring check + competitor token scan) is still unbuilt. validate_review_output remains the stub from v0. Aggregate 2.68 → 3.06. Budget note: 4 iterations remaining. Next iteration should bundle (a) voice carry-through in agent.py, which is a 10-line prose addition and cheap, with (b) the webpage_context threading + evidence substring check in service.py + gemini.py, which is the single highest-leverage remaining change and unlocks both evidence_grounding and validation_semantic_coverage simultaneously.

priority_fixes.suggestion: Thread webpage_context into run_review: in service.py:32 change 'packet, _webpage_context = build_packet_for_brief(...)' to keep the context, then pass it as a kwarg into run_review. In gemini.py add a post-parse _check_evidence_grounding(parsed, webpage_context) that (a) for each finding, checks whether any 3-word window of finding.evidence appears case-insensitively in webpage_context, or the evidence explicitly admits 'not observable / JS-rendered / dynamic'; (b) a _check_competitor_leak(parsed, brief.competitors) that string-scans the serialized JSON and rejects any brand/product token not on the allowlist or not appearing in webpage_context. On failure, build a structured Retry Note that names the offending finding.title and the specific rule violated, then loop. This is the single biggest remaining evidence-grounding lift and simultaneously bolsters validation_semantic_coverage.

In build_review_packet, immediately before '## Execution Instructions' (around agent.py:216), insert a voice reminder line: f'> Voice check: Every persona_voice and persona_reason field MUST be a first-person quote from {persona.name} using at least one of these anchors: {", ".join(persona.voice[:3])}. Auditor-voice sentences must be rewritten before finalizing.' Add a matching Reflection Loop bullet at agent.py:253: 'Voice audit: Re-read every persona_voice and persona_reason — if any could fit any generic UX audit, rewrite to use a specific voice anchor and first-person phrasing.' This exploits the voice anchors already required by ReviewBrief.validate without any schema change.

Iteration 1 delivered the prompt-level anchors. Remaining headroom is structural enforcement: (a) add a 'severity_justification' string field to each finding in review-output-schema.json so the Nielsen triad is structurally required (current schema accepts justification implicitly inside impact_on_user + impact_on_business); (b) in validate_review_output, count Blocker findings and fail validation if > max(1, len(findings)//5) unless at least one finding's problem text contains a hard-block phrase — mirrors the Reflection Loop cap as code, so it actually binds; (c) ensure improvement_direction is not a generic stub (e.g., reject if it starts with 'improve', 'enhance', 'make better' with no noun phrase). These close the gap from 4.0 toward 5.0 once the prompt-level work settles.

evaluator_feedback.feedback: Iteration 1 delivered the two highest-leverage fixes from the iter-0 priority list: temperature + top_p are now set (0.1 / 0.95), and a proper error-classification retry loop with exponential backoff + jitter + Retry-After handling is in place. retry_reliability jumps from 1.5 to 4.5 — this is excellent engineering. llm_configuration moves from 1.5 to 3.5 thanks to responseSchema being attached for 2 of 3 builders plus temperature set. However, the 'defense in depth' choice to keep the schema embedded in the prompt alongside responseSchema directly contradicts Google's controlled-generation guidance ('might lead to lower-quality generated output') and blocks both higher llm_configuration scores AND the full token_efficiency win — the schema duplication should be resolved in favor of responseSchema-only. Prompt_quality barely moved because none of the restatement duplication was addressed, the schema field-order ({score, reason} → should be {reason, score}) was not touched, and enrich_persona competitor rule is a 4th copy of a rule that should live in one place. Pipeline_structure is now active in mid-phase: duplicate webpage fetch and absent persona cache are the biggest remaining structural waste (one full HTTP crawl and often one full LLM call redundantly per end-to-end request). Recommended next iteration: (1) delete embedded schema markdown from build_review_packet, (2) reorder review-output-schema.json scores to {reason, score}, (3) consolidate competitor rule to SYSTEM_PROMPT only, (4) memoize fetch_webpage_context across persona+review stages via a simple lru_cache on URL, (5) optionally reduce to one canonical builder.

priority_fixes.suggestion: Delete the embedded schema JSON block from build_review_packet (agent.py:257-260 — the '## Required Output Schema' section with the json.dumps(schema) fenced block). Google's controlled-generation docs explicitly warn that duplicating the schema in the prompt alongside responseSchema 'might lead to lower-quality generated output'. The schema object is already attached as responseSchema for builders 2 & 3. For builder 1 (google_search grounded), replace the full schema dump with a one-line reference like 'Return JSON with these top-level keys: review_summary, persona_card, scores, strengths, findings, prioritized_improvements, open_questions.' This reclaims ~500-800 tokens per call and removes the anti-pattern.

(a) In review-output-schema.json, reorder each score object from {"score": 1, "reason": "string"} to {"reason": "string", "score": 1} (lines 24-31). This enforces reasoning-before-commit chain-of-thought ordering per structured-output best practices. Do the same reordering in any schema-generation code that mirrors the template order so propertyOrdering matches. (b) Consolidate the competitor rule to a SINGLE location: keep it ONLY in SYSTEM_PROMPT (agent.py:46-52), and delete the restatements in (i) packet 'Known Competitors' header (agent.py:195), (ii) plain-text fallback builder (gemini.py:311), (iii) enrich_persona prompt (gemini.py:360-365). The packet header can simply read 'Known Competitors (only these names may be mentioned)'. Same treatment for EVALUATE-not-EXPAND: keep only in SYSTEM_PROMPT, drop from exec instructions 7-10 and reflection loop.

Eliminate the duplicate webpage fetch: generate_persona_from_form (service.py:88) and build_packet_for_brief (service.py:18) both call fetch_webpage_context for the same URL in a single end-to-end request. Add @functools.lru_cache(maxsize=256) to fetch_webpage_context in webpage.py, OR thread the webpage_context through: have generate_persona_from_form return (persona_dict, webpage_context) and pass the context into build_packet_for_brief to skip re-fetching. Additionally, add a persona cache keyed by sha256(service_name|service_type|persona_description)[:16] that short-circuits enrich_persona on identical inputs — critical for batch evaluation workloads. Also consider a model-tiering knob: default enrich_persona to gemini-2.5-flash (service.py:84) while keeping run_review on gemini-2.5-pro, saving ~5-10x on persona cost.

Two small polish items: (1) Extend _parse_retry_after (gemini.py:53-59) to also accept HTTP-date form per RFC 7231 — use email.utils.parsedate_to_datetime and fall back to computing seconds from now; Gemini occasionally returns HTTP-date. (2) Decouple attempt index from builder index (gemini.py:86): only switch request shape on validation failures (empty/invalid JSON after success), not on API-layer transient failures — on a 503, retrying with the SAME prompt is correct; switching prompt shape confounds the root cause. Track two counters (api_attempts, validation_attempts) and pick the builder based on validation_attempts only.

evaluator_feedback.feedback: Iteration 1 applied fixes only to the LLM backend (agent.py, gemini.py) — src/personalens/webapp.py was not modified, so every web-UX issue from the v0 baseline persists unchanged. All scores hold steady (aggregate 3.315, identical to v0). The three highest-leverage unblocked items remain: (1) add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) fallback to the four inline keyframes (spin, skel-shimmer, skel-fade-in, skel-dot-anim) — the smallest diff with the largest WCAG payoff; (2) propagate data-en/data-ko attributes and a qra-lang on-load hook into render_persona_card and render_result, and update document.documentElement.lang there too, so KO persists past step 1; (3) fix the Regenerate button at L829 to actually re-POST /persona instead of location.reload(), and pause the /health poller on document.hidden with a soft recovery banner instead of unconditional window.location.reload(). None of these require architectural change — each is a ~10–30 LOC surgical edit inside webapp.py.

priority_fixes.suggestion: Add data-en/data-ko attributes and an on-load `setLang(localStorage.getItem('qra-lang') || 'en')` script to render_persona_card (L719-866) and render_result (L482-716). Also replace the hard-coded `<html lang="en">` at L593 and L739 with a runtime-updated value. A Korean user who toggled language on the form currently drops back into English for steps 2 and 3. Failing WCAG SC 3.1.1 + SC 3.1.2.

Wrap the main content of each of the three templates in `<main>`, the top section (title + language toggle) in `<header>`, and the tips sidebar in `<aside>`. Add `role="alert"` to the error <div> at L185 and `role="status" aria-live="polite"` to the polling banner (AUTO_RECONNECT_SCRIPT L24-39). Add `aria-pressed="true|false"` to the two language-toggle buttons (L355-356) and update it in setLang(). Failing WCAG SC 1.3.1 + SC 4.1.3.

Add `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .shimmer, .skel-fade-in, .skel-progress-dot, [style*="spin"] { animation: none !important; } }` to the <style> blocks covering L450 (spin), L960 (skel-shimmer), L964 (skel-fade-in), L1076 (skel-dot-anim). All four keyframes currently violate WCAG SC 2.3.3.

Fix the Regenerate button at L829: replace `onclick="location.reload()"` with an addEventListener handler that POSTs to /persona using the original form payload (stash it on a data-attribute of the form or in sessionStorage when render_persona_card is built). Add `aria-disabled="true"` and `aria-busy="true"` during the in-flight request so double-tap is harmless. Currently the button is a no-op from the user's mental model.

In AUTO_RECONNECT_SCRIPT (L18-66): (a) register `document.addEventListener('visibilitychange', …)` to clearInterval on hidden and restart on visible; (b) replace unconditional `window.location.reload()` at L49 with a soft banner update via DOM mutation + `aria-live="polite"` announcement — only force-reload if the server returns a new session token. Prevents discarding in-flight form state and battery drain on background tabs.

Add a visible required-indicator asterisk (e.g., `*` + legend text) next to labels for the 5 required inputs (service_name, service_url, service_type, core_journey, persona_description at L365-379). Disable the submit button at L400 with aria-busy="true" at the start of the form submit handler (L454-475), BEFORE calling showPersonaLoading(), to close the double-submit window.

prioritized_fixes.suggestion: Delete the embedded schema JSON block from build_review_packet (agent.py:257-260 — the '## Required Output Schema' section with the json.dumps(schema) fenced block). Google's controlled-generation docs explicitly warn that duplicating the schema in the prompt alongside responseSchema 'might lead to lower-quality generated output'. The schema object is already attached as responseSchema for builders 2 & 3. For builder 1 (google_search grounded), replace the full schema dump with a one-line reference like 'Return JSON with these top-level keys: review_summary, persona_card, scores, strengths, findings, prioritized_improvements, open_questions.' This reclaims ~500-800 tokens per call and removes the anti-pattern.

wisdom_source.strategy_name: HTTP Status Classification and Exponential Backoff with Jitter

strategy: iteration 1 gains: finding_severity_discipline +2.0 (behavioral anchors delivered), retry_reliability +3.0 (error classification + backoff delivered), llm_configuration +2.0 (responseSchema + temperature attached)

iteration 1 gaps: evidence_grounding structural lever unbuilt (webpage_context threading), persona_grounding_and_voice voice carry-through unbuilt, webapp.py untouched (all web-UX issues persist), schema duplication anti-pattern introduced

iteration 2 priorities: (1) thread webpage_context + evidence substring check — unlocks evidence_grounding and validation_semantic_coverage simultaneously; (2) voice carry-through insertion in agent.py — 10-line prose addition; (3) delete embedded schema from packet — removes anti-pattern and reclaims ~500-800 tokens; (4) webapp.py prefers-reduced-motion + lang propagation — highest WCAG payoff per LOC

applied_fixes.description: Added responseSchema + temperature=0.1 + topP=0.95 to generationConfig in inline and plain-text builders (google_search builder kept without responseSchema per Gemini API constraint); restored responseMimeType on plain-text fallback; added temperature/topP + new error-classification path to enrich_persona.

wisdom_source.strategy_name: Native Structured Output via responseSchema API Attachment

applied_fixes.description: New GeminiPermanentError / GeminiRateLimitError (with Retry-After parsing) / GeminiTransientError hierarchy; _make_request now classifies HTTP status codes; run_review capped at 3 attempts with full-jitter exponential backoff (base=1s, cap=30s); SAFETY/RECITATION/BLOCKLIST/PROHIBITED_CONTENT finishReason raises GeminiPermanentError; enrich_persona uses the same taxonomy.
applied_fixes.effect: retry_reliability 1.5 -> 4.5 (+3.0); nearly state-of-the-art per critic sources (AWS full-jitter, RFC 7231).

wisdom_source.strategy_name: HTTP Status Classification and Exponential Backoff with Jitter

applied_fixes.description: Replaced bare 'Prioritize issues using: Blocker, High, Medium, Nit.' with behavioral anchors keyed to core_journey/business_goal; required impact_on_user + impact_on_business to reconstruct Nielsen's severity triad (frequency, magnitude, persistence); added Reflection Loop blocker-cap (1 per 5) + downgrade discipline.
applied_fixes.effect: finding_severity_discipline 2.0 -> 4.0 (+2.0); secondary lifts on evidence_grounding and scoring_rubric_calibration.

wisdom_source.strategy_name: Behavioral Anchors for Severity Rating Scale
